hyde: Venules, the smallest veins in the circulation, have larger lumen diameters than arterioles, which are terminal branches of arteries. hyde: Bioavailability refers to the fraction of an administered drug dose that reaches systemic circulation and is available to produce a therapeutic effect. The bioavailability of a pharmaceutical substance describes what fraction of the ingested or administered dose becomes available at the site of action. It encompasses absorption into the bloodstream, distribution to tissues, and avoidance of hepatic metabolism. Understanding bioavailability is crucial for determining appropriate dosing regimens. Factors affecting it include drug formulation, patient age, liver function, food interactions, and concurrent medications.

To close a futures position, submit an order for the opposite trade: sell if long, buy if short, matching contract type and quantity. Once filled, your position is instantly eliminated on the exchange. Closure locks in your profit or loss at the execution price. While settlement and margin return take one to two business days, position closure is instantaneous.
